content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove clean install, generation, strict and compatibility types, lint, unit, integration, emitted smoke, browser install, and package inspection.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Clean Reproduction model · Theory Clean Reproduc…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Clean Reproduction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Clean Reproduction model · Theory Clean Reproduc…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5622 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, TypeScript, pinned crawler packages, emitted artifacts, clean gates, and recovery anchors; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove clean install, generation, strict and compatibility types, lint, unit, integration, emitted smoke, browser install, and package inspection.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Clean Reproduction model · Theory Clean Reproduc…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A hot-reload run with warm node_modules is reproducible release evidence.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-clean-reproduction-mp2o98.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-clean-reproduction-mp2o98(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_clean_reproduction_mp2o98\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cleanGates < 12)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passedGates < 12 \|\| passedGates > cleanGates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?emittedSmokes < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unresolvedVersions > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using prove and clean plus specific case evidence.
